info.text: CSIR-UGC NET Mathematical Sciences, September 2022, Shift-II (Set 1). Part A: General Aptitude (Q1-20). Marks: +2 correct, -0.5 wrong. Attempt any 15. Q18 DROPPED by NTA.

name.text: SEP2022 Q1 - Scoring test correct vs incorrect answers

questiontext.text: In a test with multiple choice questions, candidates get 4 marks for a correct answer and lose 1 mark for an incorrect answer. Two candidates A and B attempting 18 and 13 questions, respectively, secure equal marks. How many more INCORRECT answers does A have compared to B?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 4 (Option 2)

Let \(c_A,w_A\) and \(c_B,w_B\) be correct/wrong for A and B. Then \(c_A+w_A=18\), \(c_B+w_B=13\), \(4c_A-w_A=4c_B-w_B\). Subtracting: \(4(c_A-c_B)=w_A-w_B\). Also \((c_A-c_B)+(w_A-w_B)=5\). Let \(d=w_A-w_B\): \(d/4+d=5\Rightarrow d=\mathbf{4}\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q2 - Rank in class from top and bottom

questiontext.text: A ranks 10th from both the top and the bottom in merit among the girls in her class. B ranks 6th from the top and 16th from the bottom among boys in the same class. If A is immediately ahead of B in merit order, her rank in the entire class would be

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 15th from top and 26th from bottom (Option 2)

Girls \(=10+10-1=19\); Boys \(=6+16-1=21\); Total \(=40\). Girls above A \(=9\); Boys above B \(=5\). A is immediately above B, so students above A \(=9+5=14\). A's rank from top \(=15\); from bottom \(=40-15+1=26\).

answer.text: 16th from the top and 26th from the bottom
15th from the top and 26th from the bottom
15th from the top and 27th from the bottom
16th from the top and 27th from the bottom

name.text: SEP2022 Q3 - pH, CO2, Temperature graph interpretation

questiontext.text: Given figure represents pH, partial pressure of CO\(_2\) (\(p\)CO\(_2\)), and temperature (\(T\)) in an experiment conducted in a water sample over 20 days. Which of the following statements can definitely be made based on this experiment?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3

Only Option 3 directly describes what the graphs show (observational fact). Options 1, 2, and 4 imply causation or further interpretation beyond the data.

answer.text: There is a decrease in pH and an increase in both \(T\) and \(p\)CO\(_2\) over 20 days.
pH and \(p\)CO\(_2\) are positively correlated while pH and \(T\) are inversely correlated.

name.text: SEP2022 Q4 - Alcohol percentage after water addition

questiontext.text: A 360 ml aqueous solution contains 40% alcohol. How much will be the approximate percentage of alcohol if 3600 ml of water is added to the solution?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 3.6% (Option 2)

Alcohol \(=0.4\times360=144\) ml. New volume \(=360+3600=3960\) ml. \(\%=\frac{144}{3960}\times100=3.636\approx3.6\%\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q5 - Minimum weighings to find lighter coin among 27

questiontext.text: A set of 27 similar looking coins has 26 identical coins and one dummy coin having less weight. What is the minimum number of weighings that will ensure identification of the dummy coin using a two-pan balance?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 3 (Option 1)

Each weighing has 3 outcomes. \(3^3=27\). Divide into groups of 9, then 3, then 3. Three weighings suffice.

name.text: SEP2022 Q6 - Probability of end-of-row seat

questiontext.text: If 90 people are to be seated randomly in 15 rows of 6 seats each, what is the probability that a person gets a seat at either end of a row?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 1/3 (Option 3)

End seats \(=15\times2=30\). Total seats \(=90\). \(P=30/90=1/3\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q7 - Venn diagram for FOOD, VEGETABLES, ROOTS, ICECREAMS

questiontext.text: An appropriate diagram showing the relationship between the categories FOOD, VEGETABLES, ROOTS and ICECREAMS is: [Figure-based question: select the correct Venn diagram]

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option C (Option 3)

FOOD \(\supset\) VEGETABLES \(\supset\) ROOTS; ICECREAMS \(\subset\) FOOD but disjoint from VEGETABLES. Option C shows this nested + separate structure correctly.

name.text: SEP2022 Q8 - Self-referential statements consistency

questiontext.text:
Consider the following four statements:
Statement 1: "Statement 3 is true."
Statement 2: "Statement 1 is true."
Statement 3: "Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false."
Statement 4: "Statements 1, 2 and 3 are false."
Which of the above statements must be true for the four statements to be mutually consistent?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Statement 4 (Option 4)

If S4 is true: S1, S2, S3 are all false. S1 false \(\Rightarrow\) S3 false (\(\checkmark\)). S2 false \(\Rightarrow\) S1 false (\(\checkmark\)). S3 false: "S1 true AND S2 false" is false — since S1 is false, this compound statement is false (\(\checkmark\)). Self-consistent.

name.text: SEP2022 Q9 - Running race extra time calculation

questiontext.text: On a track of 200 m length, S runs from the starting point and R starts 20 m ahead of S at the same time. Both reach the end of the track at the same time. S runs at a uniform speed of 10 m/s. If R also runs at a uniform speed, then how much more time would R take to run the entire course?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 2.2 seconds (Option 4)

S: time \(=200/10=20\) s. R covers 180 m in 20 s \(\Rightarrow\) speed \(=9\) m/s. Time for R to cover 200 m \(=200/9\approx22.22\) s. Extra \(=22.22-20\approx2.2\) s.

name.text: SEP2022 Q10 - Arithmetic mean of five numbers is zero

questiontext.text: The arithmetic mean of five numbers is zero. The numbers may not be distinct. Which of the following must be true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4

Sum \(=0\). If exactly one number \(x\neq0\) and rest are 0, sum \(=x\neq0\): contradiction. So option 4 must hold. Counterexamples invalidate options 1–3.

answer.text: The product of the numbers is zero.
At most two of these numbers are positive.
There cannot be exactly one zero.
There cannot be exactly one non-zero number.

name.text: SEP2022 Q11 - Log-scale circle on linear scale

questiontext.text: A circle is drawn on \(\log_{10}(X)\) vs \(\log_{10}(Y)\) axes. The circle spans approximately \(\log_{10}X\in[0.4,1.6]\) and \(\log_{10}Y\in[0.2,1.2]\). Which one of the following, drawn on a linear scale, represents this circle?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option A (Option 1)

On a log scale, a "circle" maps to a shape compressed at smaller values. The corresponding linear-scale plot is a curved, asymmetric shape in the lower-left, wider at larger values. Option A correctly depicts this distortion.

name.text: SEP2022 Q12 - Magazine M highest readership logic

questiontext.text: Of all the English magazines published in a country, magazine M is read by the highest number of readers. It necessarily follows that

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 1

Option 1 exactly restates the premise (scope limited to magazines published in the country). Options 2–4 go beyond the given information.

answer.text: M is the most popular English magazine published in the country.
M is the most popular English magazine in the country.
M is the most popular magazine in the country.
The study has not considered the readership of English magazines published outside the country.

name.text: SEP2022 Q13 - Three digit PINs with third digit = sum of first two

questiontext.text: The number of three digit PINs, in which the third digit is the sum of the first two digits, is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 55 (Option 1)

Need \(d_1,d_2\in\{0,\ldots,9\}\) with \(d_1+d_2\leq9\). Count \(=\sum_{s=0}^{9}(s+1)=1+2+\cdots+10=55\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q14 - Right-angled triangle with equal medians, ratio BC/ML

questiontext.text: Consider a right-angled triangle BAC (right angle at A) with medians CM and BL having the same length. The ratio of the length of BC to that of ML is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 2 (Option 1)

Let \(AB=c\), \(AC=b\). \(CM=BL\Rightarrow b=c\) (isosceles right triangle). \(BC=b\sqrt{2}\). ML is the midsegment joining midpoints of AB and AC, so \(ML=BC/2\). Hence \(BC/ML=2\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q15 - Battalion elephants horses soldiers feet on ground

questiontext.text: A battalion consists of elephants, horses and soldiers totalling to 3500. There are twice as many horses as elephants and one-fourth of the soldiers are riding these animals. In the stand still position, number of feet on ground is 7500. The number of horses in the battalion is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 600 (Option 4)

\(e=\) elephants, \(h=2e\), \(s=\) soldiers. \(3e+s=3500\). Feet: \(4e+4(2e)+2\cdot\frac{3s}{4}=7500\Rightarrow12e+\frac{3s}{2}=7500\). Substituting \(s=3500-3e\): \(e=300\), \(h=600\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q16 - Market share per employee and sale per market share from graph

questiontext.text: The graph shows number of employees, market share (% by number of units sold), and sale (in Rs. crore) for five companies A, B, C, D, E. Select the CORRECT statement.

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4

From the graph: A has the highest market share per employee; B has the highest sale for its market share. (Official NTA answer = Option 4.)

answer.text: A has the highest market share per employee, C has the highest sale for its market share.
C has the highest market share per employee, B has the highest sale for its market share.
D has the highest market share per employee, E has the highest sale for its market share.
A has the highest market share per employee, B has the highest sale for its market share.

name.text: SEP2022 Q17 - Tiered wholesale pricing for 120 units

questiontext.text: The wholesale price per unit of an item is \(C_0\) up to first 19 units. The unit price falls by 10% if 20 to 29 units are purchased and by another 10% if 30 or more units are purchased. If 120 units are bought, the total price paid is approximately

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(97C_0\) (Option 2)

All 120 units at \(0.9\times0.9\,C_0=0.81\,C_0\). Total \(=120\times0.81\,C_0=97.2\,C_0\approx97\,C_0\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q18 - DROPPED - Digital clock time difference

questiontext.text:
[DROPPED by NTA — all candidates awarded full marks]

Two digital clocks show times 09h 13m and 09h 17m, respectively, at one instant. Exactly 30 seconds later the clocks show 09h 14m and 09h 17m, respectively. Which one of the following options is a possible difference between the times maintained by the two clocks?

generalfeedback.text: DROPPED by NTA. This question was officially dropped and all candidates awarded marks regardless of response.

name.text: SEP2022 Q19 - Ratio of areas of two concentric circular tracks

questiontext.text: There are two concentric circular tracks A (outer) and B (inner) of width 2 m each. If \(r=30\) m (outer radius of Track A), what is the ratio of the areas of Track A to Track B?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 29/14 (Option 3)

Per official NTA key, the answer is 29/14. (Note: Inner radius of B \(=r/2=15\) m from the given figure. Track B: \(r_\text{in}=15, r_\text{out}=17\); Track A: \(r_\text{in}=28, r_\text{out}=30\). Official answer: 29/14.)

name.text: SEP2022 Q20 - Watermelon water evaporation problem

questiontext.text: A 5 kg watermelon contains 99% water by weight. Some of the water evaporates and the melon now contains 98% water by weight. What is the weight (in kg) of watermelon now?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 2.5 kg (Option 2)

Non-water (dry matter) \(=0.01\times5=0.05\) kg (constant). After evaporation, dry matter \(=2\%\) of \(W\): \(0.02W=0.05\Rightarrow W=2.5\) kg.
